title: Site Licensing Forms - Canada.ca
content:
## Site Licensing Forms
All businesses in Canada that wish to manufacture, package, label or import natural health products for sale must hold a current site licence.
A site licence gives the licensee the authorization to manufacture, package, label or import natural health products in Canada. These activities must be carried out according to the good manufacturing practices outlined in part 3 of the Natural Health Product Regulations .
- How to interact with us electronically
- Harmonized Site Licence and FSRN Application Form

## How to interact with us electronically
As of March 30, 2026, Health Canada began using  LiquidFiles to:
- receive all site licence submissions, including new  applications, amendments,  notifications, relinquishments and renewals
- send and receive all submission-related  correspondence, including clarimails and  information request notices ( IRN )
- issue regulatory decisions for submissions,  including site licences, issuance letters and  notices of refusal
This change in process only applies to site  licensing. For NHP product licence applications, continue to  follow the process described in Electronically  contacting the Natural and Non-prescription Health Products Direct .
All site licence submissions must be submitted here:
Site Licence  Submission Gateway: LiquidFiles
You do not need a LiquidFiles account to use or  access the Gateway. To access the Gateway, you will go through a simple email authentication step for security purposes. You will be asked to “Login” each  time you use the portal. Click on the ‘Authenticate’ button and a login token will be sent to your email.
Before you apply for a site  licence, Health Canada must assign a company code (a number) for your company.
- If you do not already have a company code, email us at hpcd.nhp.sl-dcps.psn.le@hc-sc.gc.ca and put in the subject line “ Request for a company code”.
- In the body of your email, include the company information that you will be using to complete your site licence application form (Web SLA), including       company name and address, contact information and senior official (for example, chief executive officer or director).

## Harmonized Site Licence and FSRN Application Form
Released on  November 12, 2020, the Harmonized Site Licence and FSRN Application Form allows users to fill a  site licence application online. The completed  application form is saved as an .html file that must be sent to  Health  Canada as part of the submission package.
For best results, we recommend using the Harmonized Site Licence and FSRN Application form in Firefox 54+ or Chrome 60+. JavaScript must be enabled in your browser.
Instructions for completing the form can be found in the form itself:
- Harmonized Site Licence and FSRN Application Form
Please read the instructions for completing the following forms first.
- Quality Assurance Report Form (QAR) ( PDF fillable/saveable, 815 K), with instructions
- Supplementary Quality Assurance Report (SQAR) Form for Homeopathic Medicines (HM) ( PDF fillable/saveable, 481 K), with instructions
- Summary of Net Changes Form (SNC) ( PDF fillable/saveable, 381 K), with instructions
- Designated Party Authorization Form
